findingDetails.title: 015 changelog inherits review-report's internal P0-count inconsistency (claims 2 P0; report table holds 1 P0 finding F1)
findingDetails.evidence: Changelog:24 says '2 P0'; faithfully mirrors review-report.md:13 (summary table P0=2) + impl-summary.md:51. But review-report.md:41 finding table carries only ONE **P0** cell (F1). grep '**P0**' over report = 2 cells (summary row :13 + F1 row :41) = exactly 1 P0 finding.
findingDetails.recommendation: No changelog edit needed (faithful transcription). Upstream fix belongs in 015-docs-drift-review/review/review-report.md:13 summary table + implementation-summary.md:51 — reconcile 2 P0 (summary) vs 1 P0 finding (table). Route to a 015 report follow-on, not a code/changelog blocker.
findingDetails.scopeProof: changelog-000-015-docs-drift-review.md:24 (claim); 015-docs-drift-review/review/review-report.md:13 (P0=2 summary) vs :41 (only F1 = P0 finding); implementation-summary.md:51 (same headline)
findingDetails.claim: The 015 changelog's '2 P0' is not independently verifiable from packet per-finding evidence (report table holds only 1 P0 finding, F1).
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Grepped report for a second P0 finding ('**P0**','two P0','second P0','F1.*P0') — only summary-row + single F1 row found; no second P0-tagged finding.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Changelog faithfully transcribes packet headline; miscount originates in review-report summary table, not the changelog — hence P2 not P1 against A9.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: If 'consolidated' deliberately counts F1's two sub-claims (36->37 AND L6 6->7) as 2 P0 items, count is intentional -> non-finding.
